title: Executive Operating System

acceptanceScenarios.name: Create durable executive packet
acceptanceScenarios.given: The tenant has operating reports, value packets, revenue packets, autonomous loops, risk packets, trust packets, assistant audit events, and action queue items.
acceptanceScenarios.when: An executive or strategy owner opens Sprint 4 and clicks Create Sprint 4 packet.
acceptanceScenarios.then: The app persists an AssistantExecutiveOperatingPacket with board brief, investor narrative, corp-dev radar, executive twin, strategy execution, decision ledger, learning loop, operating cadence, rollback plan, and leadership summary.

acceptanceScenarios.name: Queue executive review
acceptanceScenarios.given: A Sprint 4 packet has open strategy risks, narrative risks, decision candidates, or learning signals.
acceptanceScenarios.when: The user adds a note and clicks Queue review.
acceptanceScenarios.then: The packet moves to REVIEW_QUEUED, an AssistantActionQueueItem is created, and an AssistantAuditEvent records that executive use is approval-gated.

acceptanceScenarios.name: Approve without silent mutation
acceptanceScenarios.given: A human reviewer has validated board, investor, strategy, decision, and learning evidence.
acceptanceScenarios.then: The packet is marked APPROVED with approver metadata while board materials, investor/customer communications, corp-dev commitments, strategy decisions, budgets, automation, and source records remain unchanged.

guardrails: Packet creation never publishes board materials, sends investor/customer communications, creates corp-dev commitments, changes strategy, mutates budgets, executes automation, or updates source records.
Executive outputs are decision support and draft language only until a human queues and approves downstream work.
Action queue handoff and audit events are explicit for every review or approval.
